name: Push-Up
resistance_profile: Bodyweight horizontal press; add load (vest/plate) or elevate feet to progress.
cues: Lower the chest to just off the floor
execution_steps: Set your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width with your body in a straight plank from head to heels.
Brace your core and glutes so your hips do not sag.
Bend your elbows to about 45 degrees from the torso and lower your chest to just above the floor.
Press back up to full arm extension, keeping your body rigid throughout.
good_when: You have no equipment and want a solid chest and pressing builder anywhere.
You are a beginner building baseline pressing strength.
You want a joint-friendly warm-up or a high-rep finisher.
bad_when: Bodyweight is too easy to drive progress (add a vest or switch to loaded pressing).
You cannot yet hold a rigid plank (regress to incline or knee push-ups).
